## reference · Peak-End Rule

* People judge experiences based on their peak and end moments, not the overall experience.
* Pay attention to the most intense points and final moments of the user journey.
* Identify moments when your product is most helpful, valuable, or entertaining and design to delight users.
* Negative experiences are recalled more vividly than positive ones.
* A 1993 study by Kahneman et al. showed that people prefer experiences with a better ending, even if the overall experience is longer and more uncomfortable.

Sources: https://lawsofux.com/peak-end-rule/
